
Reviewed Grok social signals and arXiv RSS signals. The arXiv papers are mostly general AI/planning research with no Claude-specific practical tips. The Grok signals include several Claude-specific resources. Three stand out as meeting the inclusion criteria: Simon Willison's hands-on computer use walkthrough (practical, evidence-grounded, broad audience, non-obvious), the Anthropic official computer use announcement (practical workflows, official source, broad), and the Awesome Claude Prompts repo (curated practical workflows). The 'Prompt Engineering for Claude 3.5 Sonnet' link points to a third-party site with unverifiable social evidence, and the Interconnects piece is more analytical than tip-focused. Ranking by signal strength: Simon Willison's post first (highest credibility, concrete techniques), then Anthropic's official computer use page (authoritative workflows), then Awesome Claude Prompts (curated community resource).

## Suggestions

- [Trying out Claude's computer use](https://simonwillison.net/2024/Oct/22/claude-computer-use/) — Simon Willison's hands-on walkthrough of Claude's computer use API with working code examples, concrete prompting patterns, and observed failure modes — the most credible practitioner write-up available.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche), Novel or non-obvious — not a basic feature already in official docs

- [New Claude 3.5 models with computer use (beta) and Claude 3.5 Haiku — Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/3-5-models-computer-use) — Anthropic's official release post covering prompting best practices, tool-use patterns, and efficiency tips for computer control workflows and the faster/cheaper Haiku model.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche)

- [Awesome Claude Prompts](https://github.com/awesome-claude/awesome-claude-prompts) — Community-maintained GitHub repo of curated, practical Claude prompt workflows updated post-3.5 release, covering a wide range of use cases with copy-paste-ready examples.
  Section: Tips of the Week | Criteria: Practical, actionable tip or technique for using Claude more effectively, Grounded in real usage evidence (community post, demo, benchmark, or documented workflow), Applicable to a broad audience (not highly domain-specific or niche)
